slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) highlights critical gaps in evidence completeness for the Q1 2024 Northwind vendor-control review, directly blocking control closure due to missing or unverified items like EV-002 and EV-003. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email confirms a formally approved, ongoing privileged access arrangement for Northwind support administrators, contingent on logging, operational constraints, and mandatory quarterly recertification to sustain compliance.

- Missing or unverified evidence items (e.g., EV-002, EV-003) prevent control closure in EVD-101.  
- Access in the Privileged Account Email is approved but requires strict adherence to logging and quarterly reviews.  
- EVD-101 evidence must include owner, review date, and control period linkage to be valid.  
- Privileged access is limited to support and break-fix activities under defined operational constraints.  
- Control closure in EVD-101 is blocked until missing evidence is resolved; access in the email remains active pending compliance.  
- Both documents tie access governance to audit readiness, but EVD-101 focuses on evidence gaps, while the email emphasizes procedural compliance for ongoing access.
